lesson: Use (?P<name>...) named groups and match.groupdict() to pull fields out by label instead of brittle numeric group(1), group(2) indices.
Prefer re.finditer over re.findall when you need match.start()/end() offsets; findall silently discards position info and collapses multi-group matches to tuples.
Chain multiple zero-width (?=...) lookaheads at the start of a pattern to require several independent conditions (uppercase, digit, min length) regardless of their order in the string.
Pass a function to re.sub so the replacement can inspect the Match object (group values, position, external counters) instead of only static backreference text.
task: snake_case to camelCase via re.sub function

lesson: Use a fixed-width negative lookbehind like (?<!\\) before a delimiter to split on it only when not backslash-escaped, then unescape the survivors.
task: split on unescaped delimiter with lookbehind

lesson: Build a lexer by alternating named groups in one compiled pattern and dispatch on match.lastgroup in finditer, instead of testing each group for None.
task: tokenizer using named-group alternation and lastgroup

lesson: Wrap a nested-quantifier subpattern like (a+)+ in an atomic group (?>a+)+ (Python 3.11+ re) to stop the engine from re-backtracking into it, killing exponential catastrophic-backtracking blowups.
task: atomic group vs catastrophic backtracking, measured

lesson: Wrap a pattern in a lookahead capturing group (?=(...)) and scan with finditer to get overlapping matches, since normal matching always consumes and skips past what it just matched.
task: overlapping matches via lookahead capture trick
